task:
Complete the API Cost Tracker CLI by implementing the remaining 2 features.
This is a continuation from costctl-003 which completed F-001..F-006.

Features to implement:

F-007: Forecast — `costctl forecast [--days N]`
  Calculate rolling daily average cost over the last N days (default: 30).
  Project weekly and monthly costs based on the average.
  Requires at least 2 days of data; print warning if insufficient.
  Output: rich Table showing daily avg, weekly projected, monthly projected.
  Tests: forecast with sample data, insufficient data warning, --days override.

F-008: Export — `costctl export [--format json|csv] [--from YYYY-MM-DD] [--to YYYY-MM-DD] [--output FILE]`
  Export filtered usage entries.
  json: JSON array of objects with all fields.
  csv: CSV with header row.
  Default format: json. Default output: stdout. --output writes to file.
  Tests: JSON schema correct, CSV has headers, file output, filtered export.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT modify features F-001..F-006. They are done.
Do NOT rewrite existing source code. Only ADD new code.
Read FEATURES.md for the authoritative feature list.
program_md:
# PROGRAM.md — API Cost Tracker CLI (costctl-004, continuation)

## Product: API Cost Tracker CLI

## Objective

Complete the costctl CLI from costctl-003 with 2 remaining features (F-007, F-008).
Maximize composite quality score through hypothesis-driven mutations.

## Mutation Scope

- Max files changed per loop: 3
- Max files created per loop: 2
- Max diff lines per loop: 200
- Max endpoint/route changes per loop: 1

## Eval Protocol

weights:
  tests: 0.35
  lint: 0.20
  typecheck: 0.15
  coverage: 0.05
  security: 0.05
  feature_coverage: 0.20

## Keep/Discard Policy

- keep_threshold: score_after > score_before
- tie_policy: discard

## Budget

- max_loops: 8
- max_wall_seconds: 14400
- max_discards_in_a_row: 3

## Stop Conditions

- target_score: 1.00
- min_improvement_delta: 0.005
- max_plateau_loops: 3
- consecutive_discards >= max_discards_in_a_row

## Hypothesis Sources

- FEATURES.md, eval failures, coverage gaps, EVALS/ledger.jsonl

## Arena Contract

- **FIRST: Read EVALS/ledger.jsonl before choosing your hypothesis.**
  Find the most recent entry. Check the "verdict" field.
  If verdict was "discard_audit", your next hypothesis MUST reduce scope (fewer files).
  If verdict was "discard_regression", check which tests broke and fix in same loop.
  Write your hypothesis as: "Last verdict: <verdict> | Response: <your adaptation>"
- This is a CONTINUATION run. Features F-001..F-006 are DONE. Do NOT modify them.
- Choose exactly one remaining feature (F-007 or F-008) or one blocking defect per loop.
- Before making changes, search the codebase. Do not assume missing implementation.
- Do not create scratch, debug, or temp files.
- Do not edit scoring files, eval scripts, or EVALS/features-baseline.json.
- Do not add, remove, reorder, or split features in FEATURES.md.
- Run the smallest relevant test first, then full eval.
- Update FEATURES.md status only for the feature you implemented.
- KEEP only if score improves and audit passes.
- If tests break, fix them in the SAME loop before moving on.
- Prefer depth (thorough tests for one feature) over breadth.
- IMPORTANT: costctl-003 had 3 consecutive discard_regression loops on F-006/F-007.
  The issue was test regressions. Be careful to not break existing tests.
  Run existing tests FIRST before adding new code.
